name: Camelot
what_it_does: Python library for extracting tables from text-layer PDFs using lattice mode (bordered tables) or stream mode (whitespace-delimited tables), with output to pandas DataFrames or CSV.
use_cases: Extract bordered financial tables (balance sheets, income statements) from PDF reports directly into pandas DataFrames
Parse whitespace-delimited tabular data from PDFs using stream mode when visible borders are absent
Batch-process a directory of regulatory or government PDF filings to extract standardized tables for comparison
Export extracted tables to CSV for downstream processing in ETL pipelines without writing custom parsing logic
Tune extraction accuracy using the accuracy and whitespace attributes to filter out low-confidence table extractions before use
not_for: Scanned image PDFs without an embedded text layer — Camelot requires a text layer; use OCR + table detection for image PDFs
General PDF text extraction outside of tables — use pdfplumber or PyMuPDF for full-document text extraction
Environments where Ghostscript cannot be installed — lattice mode has a hard dependency on the Ghostscript binary
best_when: You need to extract well-structured tables from text-layer PDFs, especially bordered tables, with direct output to pandas-compatible formats for data analysis.
avoid_when: Your PDFs are scanned images, tables lack consistent structure, or you cannot install Ghostscript on the target system for lattice mode extraction.

performance.rate_limits: None (library — bound by local CPU and memory)

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Raises descriptive exceptions for missing dependencies (Ghostscript) and invalid inputs; returns empty TableList when no tables are found rather than raising, requiring explicit length checks.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Table extraction is deterministic for the same PDF and the same flavor/settings combination.
known_agent_gotchas: Lattice mode requires Ghostscript to be installed as a system binary — its absence raises a cryptic ImportError or subprocess failure at extraction time, not at import time.
camelot.read_pdf() returns a TableList even when no tables are found; agents must check len(tables) > 0 rather than treating any return value as success.
Stream mode is sensitive to column_tol and edge_tol parameters — the defaults work poorly on tables with irregular whitespace; agents should expose these as tunable parameters rather than hardcoding.

The accuracy attribute on each extracted Table (table.accuracy) indicates parsing confidence (0–100); tables with accuracy below ~80 are often misaligned and should be flagged for review rather than used directly.
Camelot only supports Python and has no JavaScript or other language bindings — agents in Node.js or polyglot environments must call it as a subprocess or use an alternative like tabula-py.

security_score_components.security_notes: Processes PDF files locally via Ghostscript subprocess in lattice mode — validate input file paths to prevent command injection. Ghostscript itself has had CVEs historically; keep it updated.
